hypothesis: A description that leads with the data privacy story + stats anchored to savings will outperform current product-focused description, because the emotional story creates a stronger reason to switch.

changes.hero_description: After PayPal bought Honey for $4B, they started selling your shopping data. CouponSnap does the same job — finds and applies coupon codes at 1,000+ stores — without the data selling.

rationale.hero_description: Narrative-driven description. Tells the story of why CouponSnap exists in 2 sentences. The $4B reference anchors the scale of Honey's commercial incentive to sell data. Risk: longer than current — monitor bounce rate and scroll depth.
rationale.social_proof_stats: Leads with 'Zero Data Sold. Ever.' as the primary differentiator stat. Tests whether making privacy the #1 metric (rather than stores or price) increases conversion for privacy-motivated visitors.
